message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to generate the second stage of a library-chain benchmark.
Stage: lib-002-dependent
Goal: Create a second library that depends on the first library and calls its public function.
Expected artifacts: library-chain/feature (project)
Validators: feature-references-core (failure_class=api_contract), feature-builds (failure_class=build)

Existing files:
library-chain/core/src/core_lib.h
library-chain/core/src/core_lib.c
library-chain/core/main.c
library-chain/core/Makefile

I need to create a new project `library-chain/feature` that depends on `library-chain/core`.
It should call `core_add` from `core_lib.h`.
I'll create a simple C project for `library-chain/feature`.
Structure:
library-chain/feature/
  src/
    feature_lib.c
    feature_lib.h
  main.c
  Makefile

`feature_lib.h`:
```c
#ifndef FEATURE_LIB_H
#define FEATURE_LIB_H

int feature_add_extra(int a, int b);

#endif
```

`feature_lib.c`:
```c
#include "feature_lib.h"
#include "../core/src/core_lib.h"

int feature_add_extra(int a, int b) {
    return core_add(a, b) + 10;
}
```

`main.c`:
```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include "src/feature_lib.h"

int main() {
    int result = feature_add_extra(2, 3);
    printf("Feature Result: %d\n", result);
    return 0;
}
```

`Makefile`:
```makefile
CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -I.
TARGET = feature_sample

all: $(TARGET)

$(TARGET): main.c src/feature_lib.c ../core/src/core_lib.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) main.c src/feature_lib.c ../core/src/core_lib.c

clean:
	rm -f $(TARGET)
```

Wait, the validator `feature-references-core` will check if it correctly references the core library's API.
The validator `feature-builds` will check if it builds.
The paths should be relative to the run root.
So `library-chain/feature/src/feature_lib.h`, etc.

Let's verify the Makefile compilation command. It needs to include `../core/src/core_lib.c` and link it.
`$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) main.c src/feature_lib.c ../core/src/core_lib.c`
This should work.

All looks good. I will construct the JSON.
One detail: `$(TARGET)` in Makefile might be interpreted by some template engines, but it's standard Make. I'll keep it.
I'll make sure the JSON is valid.
Let's assemble.
```json
{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "library-chain/feature/src/feature_lib.h",
      "content": "#ifndef FEATURE_LIB_H\n#define FEATURE_LIB_H\n\nint feature_add_extra(int a, int b);\n\n#endif\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "library-chain/feature/src/feature_lib.c",
      "content": "#include \"feature_lib.h\"\n#include \"../core/src/core_lib.h\"\n\nint feature_add_extra(int a, int b) {\n    return core_add(a, b) + 10;\n}\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "library-chain/feature/main.c",
      "content": "#include <stdio.h>\n#include \"src/feature_lib.h\"\n\nint main() {\n    int result = feature_add_extra(2, 3);\n    printf(\"Feature Result: %d\\n\", result);\n    return 0;\n}\n"
    },
    {
      "path": "library-chain/feature/Makefile",
      "content": "CC = gcc\nCFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -I.\nTARGET = feature_sample\n\nall: $(TARGET)\n\n$(TARGET): main.c src/feature_lib.c ../core/src/core_lib.c\n\t$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) main.c src/feature_lib.c ../core/src/core_lib.c\n\nclean:\n\trm -f $(TARGET)\n"
    }
  ],
  "notes": "C library depending on core library, calls core_add, builds with Makefile."
}
```
Check escaping: `../core/src/core_lib.h` -> `\"../core/src/core_lib.h\"`
`printf(\"Feature Result: %d\\n\", result);` -> `printf(\\\"Feature Result: %d\\\\n\\\", result);`
Wait, in JSON, `\n` is newline. `\\n` is literal backslash-n.
In C code, `printf("Result: %d\n", result);` needs `\n` to be a newline in the C string, so in JSON it should be `\\n`.
Let's verify: `printf(\"Feature Result: %d\\n\", result);` -> correct.
Makefile tabs: `\t$(CC)` -> correct.
All good.
I will output the JSON.
One minor point: `$(TARGET)` in Makefile might be fine.
I'll produce the exact JSON.
Done.
